channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Patrick Mahomes calls out ex-teammate for supporting Yankees over Royals in ALDS: 'He might have to move'
item.description: Chiefs quarterback Patrick Mahomes called out his former teammate Gherig Dieter for his Yankees fandom ahead of the Yankees' playoff series vs. the Royals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark a near-unanimous choice as WNBA's Rookie of the Year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Caitlin Clark has been named the WNBA Rookie of the Year in a near-unanimous vote, giving the Indiana Fever back-to-back winners after Aliyah Boston won the honor last season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Playing with Caitlin Clark making Fever intriguing landing spot for free agents, GM says
item.description: Indiana Fever general manager Lin Dunn said she has been in contact with agents of impending free agents who are intrigued about playing with Caitlin Clark.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Big Game Bound: Bills look to get back on track but face a Texans roadblock
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS – Only two perfect teams remain in the NFL going into Week 5: the Kansas City Chiefs and the Minnesota Vikings. The league’s slate of international games will begin this week when the New York Jets and Vikings meet at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium in London.
item.dc:creator: Phil Nardiello - Senior Sports Producer, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: RSL looks to solidify playoff seeding against Minnesota
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Real Salt Lake has already clinched an MLS playoff spot, but considering RSL is 6-1-2 all-time at home in the postseason, they want that home field advantage in the first round.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Reports: Pete Rose, baseball's all-time hits leader, dead at 83
item.description: LAS VEGAS — Pete Rose, one of Major League Baseball's most polarizing athletes and the game's all-time hits leader, has died at the age of 83, according to TMZ. Rose's agent, Ryan Fiterman, confirmed the news to TMZ and said, "the family is asking for privacy at this time.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Favre tries to expand his defamation lawsuit against Mississippi auditor over welfare spending
item.description: Favre is not facing criminal charges, but he is among more than three dozen people or businesses the state is suing to try to recover misspent money through the Temporary Assistance for Needy Families program.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bucs prep for Falcons in Primetime TNF after big win over Eagles
item.description: After beating the Philadelphia Eagles the Tampa Bay Buccaneers now lead the NFC South with a 3-1 record.
item.dc:creator: C.H., WFLA, NBC

item.title: Giants fire Farhan Zaidi, Buster Posey named as replacement
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The San Francisco Giants have parted ways with President of Baseball Operations Farhan Zaidi and named Buster Posey as his replacement.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Basketball Hall of Famer Dikembe Mutombo dies at 58
item.description: Mutombo starred in the NBA for 18 seasons and is considered to be one of the greatest defensive players in the history of the sport.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, Fox 5

item.title: Henry runs for 199 yards and Jackson has 3 TDs as Ravens rout previously unbeaten Bills 35-10
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Derrick Henry was off and running from the first time he touched the ball. There was no way the Buffalo Bills were stopping the Baltimore Ravens after that.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Broncos running back Tyler Badie carted from sideline with a back injury
item.description: Denver Broncos running back Tyler Badie was placed on a back board and carted from the sideline before the start of the second quarter against the New York Jets on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utes offense struggles in 23-10 loss to Arizona
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The absence of Cam Rising finally caught up to the Utah football team, as the Utes offense produced just one touchdown and one field goal in a 23-10 loss to Arizona Saturday night at Rice-Eccles Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: White Sox beat playoff-bound Tigers 4-0, stay at 121 losses going into finale
item.description: Sean Burke pitched two-hit ball over five innings, Andrew Benintendi tied his career high with his 20th homer and the Chicago White Sox beat the playoff-bound Detroit Tigers 4-0 on Saturday to stay at 121 losses going into their season finale.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: SJSU opponent forfeits volleyball match, lawsuit alleges player is transgender
item.description: (KRON) -- Boise State University's volleyball team forfeited a match against San Jose State, the school announced on Friday. The cancellation came after one San Jose State player said in a lawsuit that one of her teammates is transgender.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Jazz management excited about youth movement
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Back in April, Jazz CEO of Basketball Operations Danny Ainge had his sights on acquiring a big-name player. "Our objective is to find a player or two, and we're we're ready to roll," Ainge said five months ago. "We're ready to go big game hunting.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Former BYU track and field sprinter dies in mountain biking crash
item.description: A former Brigham Young University track and field sprinter died Wednesday afternoon after a tragic mountain biking accident, according to a university press release.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: What's next for the Oakland Coliseum now that the A's are gone?
item.description: (KRON) -- After 57 years at the Oakland Coliseum, the Oakland Athletics played their final game there on Thursday, giving the fans something to celebrate with a 3-2 win over the Texas Rangers in front of a sell-out crowd.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Photos: Inside Oakland Coliseum for A's final game
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (KRON) -- Thursday marked an end of an era for Major League Baseball in Oakland. More than 45,000 passionate Athletics fans watched their team's final game at Oakland Coliseum.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KRON4
